content.thinking:
The current state is:
- 3 RabbitMQ pods running (rabbitmq-0, rabbitmq-1, rabbitmq-2)
- /app vhost exists
- app-queue exists as a classic queue with no policy
- No policies configured on /app

I need to set the ha-all policy on /app to mirror the classic queue across all nodes. I'll use `rabbitmqctl set_policy` to do this.

The ha-all policy should:
- Apply to all queues (pattern `.*`)
- Set `ha-mode` to `all` (mirror on all nodes)
- Target queues specifically

content.text: The cluster is healthy, /app vhost and app-queue exist, but no mirroring policy is set. I'll create the `ha-all` policy now.

input.command: kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-dr-chain-20260702-022655-cluster-a exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl set_policy ha-all ".*" '{"ha-mode":"all"}' --vhost /app --apply-to queues
input.description: Set ha-all mirroring policy on /app vhost

content.content: Setting policy "ha-all" for pattern ".*" to "{"ha-mode":"all"}" with priority "0" for vhost "/app" ...

tool_use_result.stdout: Setting policy "ha-all" for pattern ".*" to "{"ha-mode":"all"}" with priority "0" for vhost "/app" ...

content.text: Now verify all three requirements are met:

input.command: kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-dr-chain-20260702-022655-cluster-a exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_queues -p /app name type policy
